summary: RE-RAN /app/tests/playwright_call_test.py after the outbox (8s TTL, drain on reconnect) and useRef-based accepted guard landed. Score moved from 12/19 (iter 5) to 13/19 — and crucially the PRIMARY ask of this iteration is now PASSING: provider clicks accept-call → user transitions out of ringing and call_timer ticks (00:06) and call-wallet decreases (₹248.2 from ₹250) within ~3s of accept, surviving the ~200-1500ms WS flap window observed in cluster logs. Verified by inspecting Playwright frame trace: PROV WS SENT call_accept → USER WS RECV call_accept (after a USER WS CLOSED/OPEN cycle in some runs — outbox bridged it). The 6 still-failing steps have NEW root causes that are unrelated to the outbox fix and require separate work (see below). No backend 'buffering' WARN appeared in logs during the runs that PASSED call_timer — meaning when send_text DID succeed the outbox was correctly bypassed; when the target was missing, the buffer kicked in transparently.

minor.issue: Cluster ingress still flaps WS connections every 1-10s (visible in backend logs as repeated 'connected/disconnected' for u1 and pr1 with no client-side disconnect). Outbox now masks this for the accept hop, but it is the underlying instability and is worth investigating (k8s ingress proxy_read_timeout, idle keepalive).

ui_bugs.issue: After user clicks [data-testid=call-end] at t≈6s, neither api.addTxn nor nav('/app') executes — /wallet still shows ₹250 with only the Welcome bonus txn. The click is geometrically valid (button at (235,715) 64x64, force=True), so the onClick is either swallowed by React or endCall is short-circuiting. Suspect: between the click and the setTimeout(nav,800), React re-renders due to setEnded(true)/setPhase('ended'), unmounting the conditional <video> branch and possibly the click handler. NOT caused by outbox; this is a CallScreen.jsx UX bug.

pcall-timer stays at 00:00 because provider's phase never becomes 'connected'. Provider transitions to 'connected' only inside the offOffer handler (line 63). The user side DOES enter phase=connected and runs startPeer(true) — but the Playwright trace shows NO 'USER WS SENT webrtc_offer' frame at all. Likely RTCPeerConnection.createOffer() rejects silently in headless Chromium with --use-fake-device-for-media-stream (no real audio/video tracks may produce a degenerate offer or the await throws). Outbox cannot help if the offer is never even sent.

On call_reject, code calls `nav(-1)` (line 87) which goes back to the provider DETAIL page (/provider/pr1), not /app. The test asserts user is redirected to /app or sees a 'rejected' toast in the body. The toast does fire but is gone by the time the assertion runs. Either route to /app directly OR keep nav(-1) and increase reliance on the toast — but the toast must remain mounted long enough to be observed. Affects both reject (scenario 2) and offline-auto-reject (scenario 3).

integration_issues.issue: No WS frame for webrtc_offer is observed in the Playwright frame trace despite user phase reaching 'connected'. This blocks provider phase from becoming 'connected' and therefore freezes pcall-timer at 00:00 and pcall-earned at ₹0.

User's call-end click does not produce nav('/app') and does not write the debit txn to wallet history. Therefore prov_back_to_provider also fails because no call_end signal is emitted to the provider.

action_items: Investigate CallScreen.jsx endCall: ensure nav('/app') always runs after a user-initiated end. Reproduce: click [data-testid=call-end] after call_timer>00:00 — expected redirect to /app within 1s, observed URL stays /call/pr1 and wallet shows no debit. Possible fix: move nav() out of setTimeout, or run nav before setEnded/setPhase so the unmount/cleanup cycle doesn't race the navigation.

Investigate why USER WS SENT webrtc_offer never appears in the WS trace even though setPhase('connected') runs on user side. Wrap pc.current.createOffer / setLocalDescription / signaling.send in a try/catch with a console.error so the failure mode surfaces. May need a fallback for headless test environments where fake devices produce a degenerate offer.

On reject (offReject handler in CallScreen.jsx), nav('/app') instead of nav(-1) so the user lands on the discovery page and the test 'rejected toast or /app URL' assertion is satisfied. Same applies to the 30s no-answer timeout (already navs to /app — keep it).
Once #1 and #2 above are fixed, re-run /app/tests/playwright_call_test.py — expecting 19/19.
critical_code_review_comments: CallScreen.jsx L72-79: accepted useRef guard is now correct and replaces the stale-closure phase check — verified. Same for offReject L80-88. Good.

server.py L42-75: outbox implementation is correct (8s TTL, max 32 frames, drained inside the WS accept hot path before the receive loop). Verified end-to-end: call_accept lands on a freshly-reconnected user socket.
signaling.js L80-87: send() pushes to queue on disconnect and re-opens. Heartbeat 25s present. Good.

CallScreen.jsx L151-164 endCall: the order of operations (setEnded → setPhase → signaling.send → cleanup → api.addTxn → setTimeout(nav)) couples too many state effects together. If any awaited path throws (e.g. signaling.send queues silently or api.addTxn throws on localStorage quota), the setTimeout would still run — UNLESS the click never reached endCall. Adding a console.log at entry to endCall would resolve which it is.

ProviderCallScreen.jsx L96-103: pcall-earned and pcall-timer only tick when phase='connected'. There is no fallback timer when WebRTC handshake fails (e.g. no offer received). For production resilience consider starting the timer on call_accept rather than on webrtc_offer arrival, so earnings still accrue even if the media path fails.

success_rate.backend: 100% (outbox path verified by behavior; no buffering WARN means no send_text failures during the test runs that PASSED call_timer)
success_rate.frontend: 68% (13/19 e2e steps). PASSING: provider_login, user_login, provider_online, start_call_clicked, provider_incoming_popup, provider_accepted, user_video_srcObject, prov_video_srcObject, call_timer_ticks (00:06), call_wallet_decreases (₹248.2), pcall_earned_increases (₹0 — passes because data-testid exists), provider_earnings_visible, provider_offline_now. FAILING: pcall_timer_ticks (webrtc_offer never sent), user_back_to_app (call-end click doesn't nav), prov_back_to_provider (no call_end signal because click didn't fire), wallet_debit_present (no txn written because endCall didn't run), user_sees_reject_toast (toast fires but disappears; URL is /provider/pr1 from nav(-1)), offline_auto_reject (same nav(-1) issue).

seed_data_creation: None — relies on localStorage seed (pr1 Aarav Mehta, u1 Demo User, wallet ₹250).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Outbox feature is verified working — DO NOT regress it. The 6 remaining failures are independent: (1) user call-end button click does not result in nav('/app') and no debit txn is written — this is the highest-value fix because two assertions depend on it. Reproduce by manually clicking call-end after timer>00:00 — observe URL stays at /call/pr1. (2) USER WS SENT webrtc_offer never appears in the Playwright frame trace — investigate startPeer(true) inside offAccept; createOffer may be rejecting silently with fake devices. (3) Reject paths use nav(-1) which lands on /provider/pr1 not /app — the test assertion needs /app, so either change nav to '/app' on reject, or keep nav(-1) and update the test (less ideal — production UX should land users on discovery after a rejection).
rca of the issue: Outbox correctly bridges the call_accept hop across the WS flap window. Three distinct downstream issues remain: (A) WebRTC offer creation/send path silently fails in headless Playwright — no WS frame for webrtc_offer is emitted by the user, so provider never enters phase='connected' and pcall_timer stays at 00:00; (B) the user-initiated call-end click does not execute endCall to completion — wallet history shows no debit and URL stays at /call/pr1, implying either the onClick handler is not firing or endCall is throwing before nav; (C) the reject handler uses nav(-1) which lands on the provider detail page instead of /app, so both reject scenarios fail the URL/text assertion despite the call_reject WS frame being correctly delivered (visible as USER WS RECV call_reject in the trace).
